name: create-diolog-content
description: Write any content in Diolog's company voice, routed through four register personas: long-form A4 guide copy (the master files create-diolog-guides renders), articles/blog posts (investor education, IR best practice, thought leadership, announcements), website + email marketing copy, and buyer-facing business cases. Successor to the diolog-brand-voice skills, rebuilt on the create-amy-content architecture: one evidence-anchored base voice (plain and literal beats clever, the skim test, epigram budgets, verbatim positioning beats, measured confidence shown never stated, spaced hyphen never em dashes, AU English) synthesised from the team voice guide, the live diolog.app copy, and Amy's line-by-line 2026-07 review of shipped AI-drafted long-form; a review ledger that compounds future corrections; the July 2026 empirical anti-AI-writing research layer; and a deterministic voice lint with per-register format budgets. v1.0.1 adds the stylometric fingerprint extracted from ~26k words of the live diolog.com.au corpus (marketing pages, non-legal help articles, all blog articles; AI-drafted resources excluded). v1.1 adds the revision protocol (item-by-item feedback disposition - applied/overruled/routed-and-landed - plus restatement and duplication sweeps) after the guide revision validated clean on copy rules but evaporated routed design feedback, left a stale spec restatement, and duplicated an epigram across a spread; ledger entries 10-12 record the classes. v1.2, from a blind generalisation test (clean-room generation on a fresh topic, class-blind adversarial review): seeded approved sample anchors with an approval-plus-clean-lint admission rule and an accretion loop; data-shaped colour counts as a claim; scaffold slots never filled with invented psychology; countable texture budgets replace vary-by-intention; lint gains the repeated-phrase check and repeat_allowlist; ledger entries 13-15. v1.3 separates rulebook from casebook: the review ledger is maintenance-only and out of the drafting/review load path, model-facing files carry each rule with at most one generic illustration (verbatim case history lives only in the ledger), and a fresh-eyes constraint forbids document recognition from framing or substituting for a rules pass - so the skill can be tested fairly on previously reviewed material. v1.3.1: role descriptions carry their object (compressed abstraction applied to people), rewrite-from-disk guard in the revision protocol, and the fingerprint section's stale 'no fingerprint yet' prose corrected. v1.4 makes the Amy lineage explicit: the positioning-beats library is now annotated with live confirmations from her sales calls (both taglines verbatim, 2026), gains her live-confirmed beats (human in the loop, not-a-replacement/superpower, the internal-auditor metaphor for the Compliance Guardian, disclosure risk vs AI risk), and fences the retired pre-Nov-2025 'investor chat repository' positioning against regeneration. v1.4.1 generalises the naming rule: names describe contents and function, never the promised outcome (measured confidence applied to naming) - closing the reframe-the-promise loophole without storing any vetoed phrase.
